text: An additional 5mm port is placed in the patient's left upper quadrant to aid in manipulating the massively distended gallbladder, which contains stones larger than two centimeters.
The surgeon initiates a medial-first dissection of the gallbladder using robotic instruments, adapting the approach due to the challenges posed by the massively distended gallbladder.

The surgeon performs lateral dissection to mobilize the gallbladder from the cystic plate, following a medial dissection approach. The gallbladder is visibly separated from the surrounding tissue, indicating the need for an extraction incision.
The surgeon fully mobilizes the gallbladder, focusing on the artery to establish a better critical view. The robotic instruments are used to manipulate and dissect the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon completes the dissection of the gallbladder from the liver, progressing from medial to lateral, with the gallbladder now fully dissociated from the liver tissue.
The surgeon clips and transects the cystic artery, preparing to externalize the gallbladder through a small mini laparotomy incision.
